00_process_flow.title: Process Flow Diagram
00_process_flow.what_is_plotted: A conceptual block-flow diagram linking CO2 and water inputs, electrolytic formate generation, X. flavus GJ10 fermentation, nitrogen-product recovery, and SCP finishing.
00_process_flow.calculation_basis: This figure is schematic rather than numerical. It summarizes the foreground model sequence implemented in process_blocks.py and the two major product branches tracked in ScenarioCategory.
important_assumptions: Electrolyzer converts CO2 to formate and a hydrogen byproduct.
Fermentation co-produces either ammonia or bio-urea plus SCP.
Recovery technologies are abstracted into unit blocks rather than PFD-grade equipment trains.
00_process_flow.interpretation_guidance: Use this figure to orient yourself to mass and energy flow through the plant, not to read quantitative values.

01_market_viability.title: Market Viability Overview
01_market_viability.what_is_plotted: Gross and net LCOX bars across baseline scales for NH3+SCP and urea+SCP, overlaid with product market-price benchmarks.
01_market_viability.calculation_basis: Gross LCOX is total annual cost divided by sellable primary product. Net LCOX subtracts co-product credits from the same numerator. Market price points use the benchmark_primary_revenue_usd_per_y metric divided by sellable product mass.
important_assumptions: Baseline rows come from run_baseline_cases().
SCP credit is included in net LCOX when enabled in ScenarioConfig.
Incumbent market benchmarks are screening values rather than forward contract prices.
01_market_viability.interpretation_guidance: Net LCOX below the benchmark line indicates a favorable screening result at that scale under the chosen assumptions.

02_cost_structure.title: Cost Structure Waterfall
02_cost_structure.what_is_plotted: A horizontal component-by-component breakdown of the per-kg primary-product cost for a selected capacity, including credits as negative bars.
02_cost_structure.calculation_basis: Variable OPEX, fixed OPEX, and credit dictionaries from TEAResults are regrouped into high-level categories and divided by sellable primary product mass.
important_assumptions: Annualized capital is calculated with the model discount rate and plant life.
Credits can include SCP, struvite, MAP fertilizer, H2, or CO2 depending on the scenario.
Groupings are presentation-level summaries and do not alter the underlying TEA.
02_cost_structure.interpretation_guidance: Use this figure to identify the dominant cost drivers and which credits are responsible for moving gross LCOX toward net LCOX.

03_scale_margin.title: Scale-Dependent Margin to Market
03_scale_margin.what_is_plotted: Net LCOX vs capacity on a log scale alongside market-price benchmarks, with shaded profitable and unprofitable regions.
03_scale_margin.calculation_basis: Rows are grouped by ScenarioCategory from run_baseline_cases(). Each point uses net_primary_lcox_usd_per_kg and the benchmark market price for the corresponding product.
important_assumptions: Only the baseline scenario family is shown.
Capacity impacts both CapEx scaling and labor heuristics.
Market price is treated as externally fixed with no endogenous response to plant size.
03_scale_margin.interpretation_guidance: The vertical gap between market price and net LCOX is the screening margin to market; a positive gap suggests economic headroom.

04_cashflow_npv.title: Best-Method NPV and Capital Comparison
04_cashflow_npv.what_is_plotted: NPV vs scale, annual cash flow at 1,000 t/y, and capital investment vs scale for the curated best NH3 and urea recovery methods.
04_cashflow_npv.calculation_basis: The figure runs run_best_methods_grid() to compare selected recovery pathways. NPV uses the TEA cash-flow model over the configured plant life and discount rate.
important_assumptions: Only best-method families are included; expensive recovery routes are intentionally excluded.
Cash flow uses benchmark product revenues plus enabled credits.
Capital scaling uses the equipment scaling exponent and Lang-factor-based installed cost logic from tea.py.
04_cashflow_npv.interpretation_guidance: Read this figure as the high-level investment screen: which recovery/product routes scale toward positive NPV and what capital burden that requires.

05_cost_vs_gwp.title: LCA Deep-Dive and Cost vs Climate Intensity
05_cost_vs_gwp.what_is_plotted: Net GWP by accounting scenario, cost vs GWP for the most attractive negative-GWP cases, and contribution analysis for the design-basis LCA case.
05_cost_vs_gwp.calculation_basis: Panel a uses run_lca_sensitivity_grid(). Panel b uses run_best_methods_negative_gwp_grid() and retains the lowest net-LCOX negative-GWP case per curated method family. Panel c stacks LCAResults contributions normalized by primary product mass.
important_assumptions: Favorable climate cases assume renewable electricity, biogenic waste CO2, SCP carbon credit, and SCP displacement credit.
The contribution analysis shown in panel c uses the grid-power, biogenic-CO2 design basis.
Haber-Bosch lines are screening benchmarks for incumbent natural-gas and coal routes.
05_cost_vs_gwp.interpretation_guidance: Panel a shows how carbon accounting choices move the result, panel b isolates the commercially attractive negative-GWP cases, and panel c explains which burdens and credits create the net footprint.

06_sensitivity_nh3.title: NH3 Sensitivity Tornado
06_sensitivity_nh3.what_is_plotted: A one-at-a-time sensitivity tornado for NH3+SCP net LCOX at the selected scale.
06_sensitivity_nh3.calculation_basis: For each configured parameter, run_sensitivity_cases() evaluates minus-delta, base, and plus-delta scenarios. The chart displays the resulting net LCOX range around the base case.
important_assumptions: The analysis is local and one-at-a-time; it does not capture parameter interactions.
The default delta is 20 percent.
Only parameters listed in SENSITIVITY_PARAMS for ScenarioCategory.AMMONIA_SCP are varied.
06_sensitivity_nh3.interpretation_guidance: The longest bars identify the assumptions to which NH3 economics are most sensitive in this screening model.

07_sensitivity_urea.title: Urea Sensitivity Tornado
07_sensitivity_urea.what_is_plotted: A one-at-a-time sensitivity tornado for urea+SCP net LCOX at the selected scale.
07_sensitivity_urea.calculation_basis: This uses the same run_sensitivity_cases() machinery as the NH3 tornado, but with the urea-specific sensitivity parameter list.
important_assumptions: The analysis is local and one-at-a-time rather than probabilistic.
The default delta is 20 percent.
Only parameters listed in SENSITIVITY_PARAMS for ScenarioCategory.BIO_UREA_SCP are varied.
07_sensitivity_urea.interpretation_guidance: Use this panel to see which technical or economic assumptions most strongly move the urea route’s net LCOX.

08_executive_summary.title: Executive Summary
08_executive_summary.what_is_plotted: A four-panel summary of net LCOX, GWP, capital investment, and NPV as a function of scale for NH3+SCP and urea+SCP baseline cases.
08_executive_summary.calculation_basis: Each panel reuses the baseline ScenarioEvaluation rows and plots one key metric from TEAResults or LCAResults across the modeled capacities.
important_assumptions: This figure uses the default baseline scenarios rather than all recovery alternatives.
The GWP panel reflects the current baseline LCA assumptions in ScenarioConfig.
NPV uses the same annual cash flow and discounting approach as fig 04.
08_executive_summary.interpretation_guidance: This is the fastest way to compare the two product platforms across the main economic and environmental KPIs.

09_nh3_recovery_compare.title: NH3 Recovery Method Comparison
09_nh3_recovery_compare.what_is_plotted: Gross and net LCOX by NH3 recovery method plus a recovery cost-driver breakdown at a fixed capacity.
09_nh3_recovery_compare.calculation_basis: Rows come from run_recovery_comparison(ScenarioCategory.AMMONIA_SCP, capacity_tpy). TEA component costs are normalized by NH3-equivalent product mass.
important_assumptions: All listed NH3 recovery methods are evaluated at the same plant scale.
The right panel uses TEA cost categories as a proxy for recovery cost drivers.
Struvite and MAP routes monetize fertilizer products instead of bulk ammonia.
09_nh3_recovery_compare.interpretation_guidance: Use this comparison to understand the trade-off between lower energy intensity, reagent demand, and credit structure across NH3 recovery options.

10_urea_recovery_compare.title: Urea Recovery Method Comparison
10_urea_recovery_compare.what_is_plotted: Gross and net LCOX, steam-versus-electricity trade-off, and GWP by urea recovery route at a fixed capacity.
10_urea_recovery_compare.calculation_basis: Rows come from run_recovery_comparison(ScenarioCategory.BIO_UREA_SCP, capacity_tpy). The center panel isolates recovery utility costs while the right panel compares LCA intensity for the same method set.
important_assumptions: The same product scale is used across all recovery routes.
The comparison is screening-level and uses simplified utility partitioning for the center panel.
MVR and evaporation are the primary routes of interest; the hybrid case is retained mainly for comparison and backward compatibility.
10_urea_recovery_compare.interpretation_guidance: This figure helps decide whether reduced steam demand and changed electricity usage justify the extra complexity of MVR-type recovery.
